examples.title: BMW Segregation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to determine correct color-coded container for biomedical waste
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SegregateBMW(wasteType):
 IF wasteType IS "Human anatomical" OR "Soiled" OR "Microbiological" OR "Discarded linen" THEN
 RETURN "YELLOW Container"
 ELSE IF wasteType IS "Plastic" OR "Gloves" OR "Tube" OR "Catheter" THEN
 RETURN "RED Container"
 ELSE IF wasteType IS "Needle" OR "Syringe" OR "Broken glass" OR "Sharp metal" THEN
 RETURN "WHITE Container"
 ELSE IF wasteType IS "Glass vial" OR "Ampoule" OR "Metallic implant" THEN
 RETURN "BLUE Container"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Invalid waste type"
 END IF
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm demonstrates how to classify biomedical waste into appropriate color-coded containers based on the BMW Rules 2016 categories.

examples.title: BMW Treatment Selection Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to select appropriate treatment method based on waste category
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SelectTreatment(wasteCategory):
 SWITCH wasteCategory:
 CASE "Yellow":
 IF facility.hasIncinerator THEN
 RETURN "Incineration"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Deep Burial"
 END IF
 CASE "Red":
 RETURN "Autoclaving OR Microwave treatment"
 CASE "White":
 RETURN "Autoclaving OR Chemical treatment OR Encapsulation"
 CASE "Blue":
 RETURN "Disposal in secure landfill"
 DEFAULT:
 RETURN "Consult SPCB"
 END SWITCH
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm helps healthcare facilities select appropriate treatment methods based on the waste category and available infrastructure.
